args: {"wheel":"s2s3","worktree":"/home/polaris/wt/s2s3","branch":"bot/s2s3-box","progress_file":"/home/polaris/wt/s2s3/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md","max_rounds":"8","mission":"Fix S2/S3 SELECT-AND-WEIGHT so the corpus metadata is CORRECT at the source. PROVEN ROOT CAUSE: the cp3 basket corpus has good, full-text, high-quality sources (median 2.5k-char quotes, e.g. Acemoglu-Restrepo JEP full text) but S2/S3 leaves their metadata BLANK and mis-tiers them. Measured on the real 329-basket corpus: only 3/995 rows had a DOI, 3/995 a journal name, and 253/995 were tier=UNKNOWN — even though a downstream repair (scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py in the outline_agent worktree, proven) recovered DOI 3->240, journal 3->294, T1+T2 47->134, UNKNOWN 253->145, and found 83 DUPLICATE rows — all from metadata ALREADY in the content/URL. That repair is a DOWNSTREAM BAND-AID; the real fix belongs IN S2/S3 so every run produces correct metadata natively. Rule of law CLAUDE.md §-1.3: weight-and-consolidate, NEVER filter/delete — only POPULATE metadata, RE-TIER on real signal, and MERGE true duplicates.","success_metric":"Re-run S2/S3 on the real corpus and produce a cp3 basket snapshot where: (a) DOI populated on >=200 rows (from regex over direct_quote+url), (b) journal/venue populated on >=250 rows (from content patterns + a defensible domain->venue map), (c) tier=UNKNOWN cut from 253 to <=150 by classifying on real signals (DOI present, known-journal domain, gov/IGO domain), NEVER downgrading a real tier, (d) the 83 true duplicates (same DOI/url/title) MERGED via the existing consolidation path (not deleted-as-filter — merged as corroboration). Prove each with a before/after count. The repair-script numbers are the FLOOR — S2/S3 doing it natively should match or beat them.","state":"worktree /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 @ HEAD 7b72d41 (branch bot/s2s3-box, s2s3 re-pass iter 7). This branch already does line-screen (S2) + finding-dedup/consolidation (S3). The reference implementation to port IN is /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py (DOI regex 10.\\d{4,9}/..., JOURNAL_PAT for 'Journal of ...' etc., DOMAIN_TIER + DOMAIN_VENUE maps) — read it, it is proven on the real corpus. Real corpus to test against: /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json (329 baskets, 995 rows) and the raw rows upstream. Find where S2 stamps evidence rows (the line-screen stage) and where S3 consolidates/dedups baskets — that is where metadata extraction + tiering + dedup must live.","next_actions":"STEP 1: read scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py in the outline_agent worktree (the proven recovery logic) and find the S2 line-screen stamp site + the S3 consolidation/dedup site in THIS worktree (grep for tier, direct_quote, basket consolidation, member merge). STEP 2: wire metadata extraction INTO S2 stamping — populate doi (regex over direct_quote+url), journal/venue (content pattern + domain map), and tier (DOMAIN_TIER + 'has DOI => >=T2'), only upgrading UNKNOWN/empty on a clear signal, never downgrading. STEP 3: wire true-duplicate MERGE into S3 consolidation (same DOI/url/title -> merge as corroboration per §-1.3, not delete). STEP 4: re-run S2/S3 on the real corpus, produce a corrected cp3, and MEASURE the before/after (DOI, journal, UNKNOWN, dupes) vs the repair-script floor. Commit each step. Keep runs small/deterministic where possible (the metadata extraction is deterministic, no LLM needed). GOAL: a corrected corpus ready to hand to the outline wheel ASAP."}

result.gap_to_top: None — all four targets beat both the target and the repair-script floor, verified by independent recount from the artifacts: DOI 5->241 (>=200, floor 240), journal 5->339 (>=250, floor 294), UNKNOWN 253->136 (<=150, floor 145), duplicates 90 detected / 126 members merged in 71 same-work groups via the existing consolidation path with all member ids+urls preserved (corroboration, not deletion). Pool-independence proven: enriching the FULL unscreened 999-row raw corpus offline gives DOI 273 / journal 350 / UNKNOWN 138 with 0 tier downgrades, so neither the --stub screen's 63 drops nor --no-nli affects the metric.

timeline.next: SIGN OFF and hand off. Answer to Opus's question: the --stub S2 + --no-nli S3 cp3 is acceptable as the corrected corpus — do NOT burn a GPU/paid run for this wheel; the metadata metric is proven pool- and NLI-independent (full-999 unscreened offline enrichment: doi=273 journal=350 UNKNOWN=138, 0 downgrades) and the same-work merge is deterministic. One final action: append a handoff caveat line to /home/polaris/wt/s2s3/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md stating (1) cp3 at outputs/s2s3_meta/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json was produced with --stub/--no-nli and a production-fidelity real-LLM+NLI run would change WHICH rows survive but not the enrichment (cite the full-999 numbers as proof), and (2) optionally delete the dead _TIER_RANK dict at src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py:72; then commit.

logs: [s2s3] r1 GATE: SIGN_OFF | at_top=true | SIGN OFF and hand off. Answer to Opus's question: the --stub S2 + --no-nli S3 cp3 is accep
summary: Opus-grinds / Fable-scalpel wheel: Opus does ALL hard work (read, search, build, fix, measure) toward a benchmark target; Fable is called ONCE per round for a precise deep-think gate only — never for hard read/search. Loops until Fable gates it top.

phases.title: Work
phases.detail: Opus: read the named files, apply the next concrete step, exercise it, commit, MEASURE the benchmark

phases.title: Gate
phases.detail: Fable: precise deep-think on the diff + measurement — correct? faithfulness-safe? top yet? exact next step
